strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and partial RELRO. The main function reads 512 bytes into a 16-byte buffer, which suggests a stack overflow vulnerability. We can use ROP to call the 'shell' function, which in turn calls 'system'. We need to find the offset to control the return address and identify gadgets to set up the ROP chain. We will also need to leak a libc address to calculate the base address of libc.
